reasoning: The LLM evaluation indicates strong content preservation with only minor visual emphasis loss. However, automated programmatic checks identified three major structural and accessibility issues: retained header/footer text across 5 pages, a roman numeral list incorrectly converted to an alphabetical list, and incomplete alternative text for 2 images. Because there are 3 major programmatic defects, the document requires human review to correct these issues.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 5; Page 3 of 5
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. March 1, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=1. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 2 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (2 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "... dark blue, with a white evergreen tree silhouette"; p1: "...m olive green on the left to dark navy blue on the"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=2 (truncated=2, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

In the source PDF, the topics at the beginning of each list item on pages 3 and 4 (such as "Beneficial Reuse Areas", "Costs", "Dam Safety", etc.) are both bolded and underlined. The main heading "MEMORANDUM" on page 1 is also underlined. The output fails to preserve any underline emphasis for these elements.
concerns.output_quote: Beneficial Reuse Areas – There was a concern about contamination

action_items: Remove retained header and footer text from the HTML.
Correct the list formatting to properly use roman numerals instead of alphabetical markers.
Complete the alternative text descriptions for the truncated image alt text.
Restore missing underline formatting to the main heading and list items if required.
